title: 10 best herbal creams for acid reflux

remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory properties, which helps to reduce inflammation in the esophagus and alleviate symptoms of acid reflux such as heartburn and regurgitation.

Flavonoids: Herbal licorice creams contain flavonoids, which have antioxidant properties that help to neutralize free radicals and protect the mucous membranes in the esophagus from damage caused by acidic stomach contents.

Saponins: Saponins, a type of glycoside found in herbal licorice creams, have a soothing effect on the digestive tract, helping to reduce inflammation and irritation in the esophagus and alleviate symptoms of acid reflux.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice, creams helps with acid reflux because of its anti-inflammatory properties and ability to soothe the digestive system. The root of the licorice plant contains a compound called glycyrrhizin, which has been shown to reduce inflammation in the esophagus and alleviate symptoms of acid reflux. Additionally, licorice creams can help protect the mucous membranes in the throat and esophagus, preventing acid from causing damage and discomfort. This natural remedy offers a gentle and effective solution for acid reflux sufferers.
remedy_constituents: Licorice Flavonoids: Licorice flavonoids, particularly glabridin and glabrene, help with acid reflux by inhibiting the production of stomach acid and reducing inflammation in the esophagus.
Glycyrrhizin: Glycyrrhizin helps with acid reflux by blocking the action of an enzyme that breaks down the protective mucous lining in the esophagus, thereby preventing acid reflux.
Glycyrrhetic Acid: Glycyrrhetic acid helps with acid reflux by reducing inflammation and preventing oxidative stress in the esophagus, which can contribute to acid reflux symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Glycyrrhizin is the primary active compound extracted from the roots, which helps in reducing inflammation and alleviating symptoms of acid reflux.
Roots: Licorice root extracts, containing glycyrrhizin, have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and protect the stomach lining from acid reflux.
Roots: Glycyrrhizin in licorice root extracts also helps in reducing the production of stomach acid, providing relief from acid reflux symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Extract 40 grams of dried roots in a 1:5 ratio with water at 70-80 degrees celsius for 30 minutes.
Filter the extract using a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove impurities immediately.
Combine the filtered extract with 10 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture in the double boiler at 60-70 degrees celsius for 20 minutes or until fully incorporated.
Pour the mixture into sterile containers and allow it to cool and solidify completely.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach lining, which can alleviate symptoms of acid reflux.

Digestive enzymes inhibitors: The phenolic compounds in ginger cream inhibit the production of digestive enzymes that break down food, allowing for a slower digestion process and reducing the risk of acidic stomach contents flowing back up into the esophagus.
Antacid properties: Ginger's antacid properties help neutralize excess stomach acid and reduce inflammation in the esophageal lining, providing quick relief from heartburn and acid reflux symptoms.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, creams helps with acid reflux because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties and ability to relax the muscles in the digestive tract. The ginger content in these creams stimulates digestion, reducing the production of stomach acid and alleviating symptoms of acid reflux. Additionally, ginger's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce inflammation in the esophagus, providing relief from heartburn and discomfort associated with acid reflux. This natural remedy promotes a soothing and calming effect on the digestive system.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These lipophilic compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach, alleviating acid reflux symptoms.
Shogaols: Similar to gingerols, shogaols exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that soothe the digestive tract, reducing acid production and alleviating acid reflux discomfort.

6-Gingerol: This gingerol derivative has been shown to have a direct inhibitory effect on gastric acid secretion, reducing the amount of acid produced in the stomach and providing relief from acid reflux symptoms.
remedy_parts: Stems: The stems of Zingiber officinale are used to make creams for acid reflux because they contain antioxidants that can help protect the digestive system from damage caused by acid reflux.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 1/4 cup of dried zingiber officinale in 2 cups of water for 10 minutes at 212°f.
Strain the infusion and discard the solids after 10 minutes of straining time.
Mix 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Combine the infused liquid and the melted beeswax mixture in a heat-proof bowl.
Whip the mixture with a hand mixer for 5 minutes until it thickens and cools.

remedy_recipe: Infuse dried tilia platyphyllos flowers in a carrier oil such as sweet almond oil at a 1:3 ratio for 2 hours.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container.
Mix the infused oil with beeswax and shea butter in a double boiler at a 2:1:1 ratio by weight.
Heat the mixture in the double boiler at 160-180°f for 10-15 minutes or until fully melted.
Pour the melted cream into a glass jar and allow it to cool and solidify at room temperature.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh matricaria chamomilla flowers on a sunny day in july for best potency.
Dry the harvested flowers at 40°c for 2 hours to preserve their active ingredients completely.
Infuse 20g of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers in 200ml of cold carrier oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil using cheesecloth and discard the solids to obtain a smooth liquid base.
Mix 100g of the strained oil with 50g of beeswax and 20g of emulsifying wax at 60°c.

Dry the harvested mentha x piperita leaves and flowers at 40°c for 2 days to remove moisture.
Infuse 200g of dried mentha x piperita in 1l of carrier oil at room temperature for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil and mix it with 250g of beeswax and 50g of coconut oil to create a smooth consistency.
Blend the mixture and pour it into a mold to set for at least 30 minutes before removal.

Combine the powdered althaea officinalis with 100g of beeswax, 50g of coconut oil, and 20g of vitamin e oil in a double boiler.

Steep 20 grams of dried foeniculum vulgare in 500 milliliters of carrier oil at 60 degrees celsius for 2 hours.

Strain the coriandrum sativum mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container, discard the solids.
Mix the coriandrum sativum infusion with 200g of shea butter and 50g of beeswax, heat the mixture in a double boiler.

Melt the mixture in the double boiler for 5 minutes while stirring occasionally to achieve a smooth consistency.

Combine 200g of dried rosa damascena flowers with 200g of jojoba oil in a double boiler.

Combine ingredients by mixing the olive oil and lemon balm leaves in a double boiler for 30 minutes.

Crush 20 grams of dried centaurea cyanus flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the powdered herb with 100 grams of beeswax pellets, 50 grams of coconut oil, and 25 grams of shea butter in a double boiler.

remedy_recipe: Gather 10 grams of dried echinacea angustifolia root powder and 20 grams of beeswax.
Infuse the powder in 100 milliliters of cold-pressed sweet almond oil for eight hours.
Mix 50 grams of coconut oil and 5 grams of shea butter with the infused oil blend.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 180 degrees fahrenheit for 20 minutes to melt the beeswax and emulsify the ingredients.
Allow the cream to cool and thicken before pouring into sterilized jars.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 grams of dried vitis vinifera leaves from organic sources in early summer months for optimal potency.

Strain the infused liquid through cheesecloth and filter out any remaining plant particles before combining with beeswax and emulsifiers.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 200g of fresh taraxacum officinale leaves and stems, rinse thoroughly with cold water.
Combine the harvested plants with 500ml of distilled water in a saucepan, simmer gently for 20 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass bowl, pressing on solids to extract liquid.
Mix 100g of beeswax and 50g of coconut oil with the extracted plant liquid, heat in a double boiler until melted.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for acid reflux are topical treatments containing plant extracts that help alleviate the discomfort and inflammation associated with acid reflux. These creams work by soothing the esophageal lining, reducing inflammation, and promoting healing. The best herbal creams for acid reflux include Glycyrrhiza glabra, Zingiber officinale, Foeniculum vulgare, Aloe barbadensis, Cinchona officinalis, Mentha x piperita, Curcuma longa, Aloe vera, Zanthoxylum bungeanum, and Althaea officinalis. If left untreated, acid reflux can lead to severe discomfort, digestive issues, and even long-term damage to the esophagus, negatively impacting daily life, sleep, and overall well-being.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to acid reflux that are treated with herbal creams are eczema, psoriasis, and skin irritation. These conditions often share symptoms such as itching, burning, and redness, which can be alleviated by topical application of herbal creams. Herbs like aloe vera, tea tree oil, and chamomile have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that help calm the skin and provide relief from discomfort.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with acid reflux is Aloe vera and Slippery Elm. Aloe vera soothes the esophagus and reduces inflammation, while Slippery Elm forms a protective barrier to prevent stomach acid from irritating the esophageal lining. When combined with Peppermint oil, which relaxes the muscles and reduces symptoms of heartburn, and Licorice root, which has anti-inflammatory properties, these creams can provide a triple threat against acid reflux, promoting faster healing and relief.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Complementary therapies in clinical practice, a randomized double-blind controlled trial was conducted on 70 subjects with gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD) to assess the effectiveness of rose oil softgels compared to omeprazole capsules. The study used the Mayo-gastroesophageal reflux questionnaire (GERQ) to collect data within three sessions of visit. The results showed that the consumption of rose oil soft capsules alleviated cardinal GERD symptoms, similar to omeprazole, suggesting that rose oil-based herbal creams could be a beneficial treatment option for acid reflux, offering a different mechanism of action without the side effects associated with proton-pump inhibitors.
